# Voss

> Voss is a local control plane for coding agents: scoped tools, code-aware recall, live workspace supervision, approvals, independent review, and replayable audits.

## Primary Pages

- [Home](https://www.tryvoss.dev/): Product overview for Voss, installation paths, and the terminal-native workflow.
- [Harness](https://www.tryvoss.dev/harness): Details on the coding harness, modes, tools, sessions, semantic recall, and bounded repository work.
- [Agentic Development Environment](https://www.tryvoss.dev/ade): Overview of the local ADE surface for live runs, approvals, sessions, replay, and inspection.
- [Language](https://www.tryvoss.dev/language): Introduction to the .voss control language for confidence, context budgets, semantic routing, and agent composition.
- [About](https://www.tryvoss.dev/about): Product positioning, project intent, and the boundaries of what Voss is and is not.
- [Contact](https://www.tryvoss.dev/contact): Public channels for product questions, documentation issues, bug reports, and security report routing.
- [Security](https://www.tryvoss.dev/security): Security model for harness permissions, local credentials, project boundaries, and execution safety.
- [Privacy](https://www.tryvoss.dev/privacy): Current marketing-site data collection posture, consent storage, and opt-in analytics behavior.
- [Security Disclosure](https://www.tryvoss.dev/disclosure): Guidance for reporting security issues without exposing sensitive details publicly.
- [Roadmap](https://www.tryvoss.dev/roadmap): Current product direction across live local supervision, semantic code recall, managed project instructions, external-agent handoffs, budget-aware context, and global memory.

## Documentation

- [Documentation Home](https://docs.tryvoss.dev): Technical documentation for installing, configuring, and using Voss.
- [Quickstart](https://docs.tryvoss.dev/get-started/quickstart): Fast path for installing Voss and running the first bounded repo task.
- [Harness Overview](https://docs.tryvoss.dev/harness/overview): How the Voss harness runs model sessions inside explicit project and permission boundaries.
- [Harness Commands](https://docs.tryvoss.dev/harness/commands): CLI command reference for common Voss harness workflows.
- [Project Cognition](https://docs.tryvoss.dev/harness/project-cognition): Durable project memory and rebuildable code recall state.
- [Harness Modes](https://docs.tryvoss.dev/harness/modes): Explanation of plan, edit, and auto permission modes.
- [Harness Tools](https://docs.tryvoss.dev/harness/tools): Read/write/shell/git tool surface exposed to Voss sessions.
- [Language Overview](https://docs.tryvoss.dev/language/overview): Overview of the .voss language and compiler/runtime direction.
- [Security Overview](https://docs.tryvoss.dev/security/overview): Security posture and operational boundaries for using Voss.
- [Latest Changelog](https://docs.tryvoss.dev/changelog/2026-06-12): Semantic code recall, budget-aware context, managed sync checks, and SDK proof-suite work.

## Developer Resources

- [GitHub Repository](https://github.com/voss-lang/voss): Source repository, issue history, and implementation details for Voss.
- [Product Requirements Document](https://github.com/voss-lang/voss/blob/master/PRD.md): Product requirements and background context for Voss.

## Optional

- [Roadmap Documentation](https://docs.tryvoss.dev/roadmap/v0-1): Detailed v0.1 roadmap documentation.
- [Troubleshooting Reference](https://docs.tryvoss.dev/reference/troubleshooting): Diagnostic guidance for common install, auth, and runtime issues.
